title: Concilio Tridentino, Sessio VII, De Baptismo
titleEn: Council of Trent, Session 7, Decree on Baptism
source: Per baptismum in Christo incorporamur et eius corpus efficimur. Sacramentum baptismi necessarium est ad salutem, tamquam sine quo nemo ad regnum Dei pervenire potest.
sourceEn: By baptism we are incorporated into Christ and become his body. The sacrament of baptism is necessary for salvation, since without it no one can attain the kingdom of God.
excerpt: 이 세례 안에서 우리는 그리스도 안으로 편입되어 그의 몸이 됩니다. 세례 성사는 구원에 필수적이며, 이것이 없이는 아무도 하느님의 나라에 이를 수 없습니다.
excerptEn: By this baptism we are incorporated into Christ and become his body. The sacrament of baptism is necessary for salvation, since without it no one can attain the kingdom of God.
provenance: Catholic Church, Council of Trent, Session 7, Canons on Baptism (1547). Authoritative conciliar teaching of the Roman Catholic Church.

title: Православный Катехизис Филарета, О Крещении
titleEn: Catechism of Philaret (Drozdov), On Baptism
source: Крещение есть таинство, в котором человек умирает для греха и возрождается к новой духовной жизни. Внешний обряд состоит в троекратном погружении в воду.
sourceEn: Baptism is the mystery in which a man dies to sin and is reborn to new spiritual life. The outward rite consists in a triple immersion in water.
excerpt: 세례는 사람이 죄에 대해 죽고 새로운 영적 생명으로 다시 태어나는 신비입니다. 외적 의식은 물에 세 번 침수하는 것으로 이루어집니다.
excerptEn: Baptism is the mystery in which a man dies to sin and is reborn to new spiritual life. The outward rite consists in a triple immersion in water.
provenance: Philaret (Drozdov), Metropolitan of Moscow, Orthodox Confession of the Catholic and Apostolic Eastern Church (expanded catechism, 1830). Authoritative Orthodox catechetical text.

title: Confessio Fidei Westmonasteriensis, Caput XXVIII
titleEn: Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 28
source: Baptismus est sacramentum Novi Testamenti, a Domino Iesu institutum, non solum ut distinguat visibiliter ecclesiam ab extraneis, sed etiam ut signaculum foederis gratiae.
sourceEn: Baptism is a sacrament of the New Testament, ordained by Jesus Christ, not only for the solemn admission of the party baptized into the visible Church, but also to be unto him a sign and seal of the covenant of grace.
excerpt: 세례는 새 언약의 성례이며, 주 예수 그리스도께서 제정하셨습니다. 이것은 세례 받는 자를 보이는 교회에 엄숙히 받아들일 뿐만 아니라, 은혜 언약의 표와 인이 됩니다.
excerptEn: Baptism is a sacrament of the New Testament, ordained by Jesus Christ, not only for the solemn admission of the party baptized into the visible Church, but also to be unto him a sign and seal of the covenant of grace.
provenance: Westminster Assembly, Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 28: Of Baptism (1646). Reformed confessional standard adopted by Presbyterian and Reformed churches worldwide.

title: Confessio Augustana, Articulus IX
titleEn: Augsburg Confession, Article IX
source: De baptismo docent quod sit necessarius ad salutem, quodque per baptismum offeratur gratia Dei, et quod baptizandi sint infantes, qui per baptismum Deo offerantur et recipiantur in gratiam Dei.
sourceEn: Of Baptism they teach that it is necessary for salvation, that by Baptism the grace of God is offered, and that children are to be baptized, who, being offered to God through Baptism, are received into God's grace.
excerpt: 세례에 대해 그들은 세례가 구원에 필요하며, 세례를 통해 하나님의 은혜가 주어진다고 가르칩니다. 아이들도 세례를 받아야 하며, 세례를 통해 하나님께 바쳐지고 하나님의 은혜 안에 받아들여집니다.
excerptEn: Of Baptism they teach that it is necessary for salvation, that by Baptism the grace of God is offered, and that children are to be baptized, who, being offered to God through Baptism, are received into God's grace.
provenance: Philip Melanchthon, Augsburg Confession, Article IX: Of Baptism (1530). Primary Lutheran confessional document presented to Emperor Charles V.

title: The Baptist Faith and Message, Article VII
titleEn: Baptist Faith and Message 2000, Article VII: Baptism and the Lord's Supper
source: Christian baptism is the immersion of a believer in water in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. It is an act of obedience symbolizing the believer's faith in a crucified, buried, and risen Saviour.
sourceEn: Christian baptism is the immersion of a believer in water in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. It is an act of obedience symbolizing the believer's faith in a crucified, buried, and risen Saviour.
excerpt: 기독교 세례는 아버지와 아들과 성령의 이름으로 물에 신자를 침수시키는 것입니다. 이것은 십자가에 못 박히시고 묻히시고 부활하신 구주에 대한 신자의 믿음을 상징하는 순종의 행위입니다.
excerptEn: Christian baptism is the immersion of a believer in water in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. It is an act of obedience symbolizing the believer's faith in a crucified, buried, and risen Saviour.
provenance: Southern Baptist Convention, The Baptist Faith and Message, Article VII (2000). Official doctrinal statement of the Southern Baptist Convention.

title: Articles of Religion, Article XVII
titleEn: Articles of Religion (Methodist), Article XVII: Of Baptism
source: Baptism is not only a sign of profession and mark of difference whereby Christians are distinguished from others that are not baptized; but it is also a sign of regeneration or the new birth.
sourceEn: Baptism is not only a sign of profession and mark of difference whereby Christians are distinguished from others that are not baptized; but it is also a sign of regeneration or the new birth.
excerpt: 세례는 단지 신앙고백의 표시이자 그리스도인을 세례 받지 않은 다른 사람들과 구별하는 표징일 뿐만 아니라, 거듭남이나 새로운 출생의 표징이기도 합니다.
excerptEn: Baptism is not only a sign of profession and mark of difference whereby Christians are distinguished from others that are not baptized; but it is also a sign of regeneration or the new birth.
provenance: John Wesley, Articles of Religion (Methodist), Article XVII: Of Baptism (1784). Adapted from the Anglican Thirty-Nine Articles for the Methodist Episcopal Church.

title: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 6
titleEn: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 6: The Ordinances of the Church
source: We believe in water baptism by immersion, and all who repent and believe on Christ as Saviour and Lord are to be baptized. Thus they declare to the world that they have died with Christ and that they also have been raised with Him to walk in newness of life.
sourceEn: We believe in water baptism by immersion, and all who repent and believe on Christ as Saviour and Lord are to be baptized. Thus they declare to the world that they have died with Christ and that they also have been raised with Him to walk in newness of life.
excerpt: 우리는 침수에 의한 물 세례를 믿으며, 회개하고 그리스도를 구주와 주로 믿는 모든 사람은 세례를 받아야 합니다. 이를 통해 그들은 그리스도와 함께 죽었고 새 생명으로 행하기 위해 그리스도와 함께 부활했음을 세상에 선포합니다.
excerptEn: We believe in water baptism by immersion, and all who repent and believe on Christ as Saviour and Lord are to be baptized. Thus they declare to the world that they have died with Christ and that they also have been raised with Him to walk in newness of life.
provenance: General Council of the Assemblies of God, Statement of Faith, Article 6 (adopted 1916, reaffirmed 2014). Official doctrinal standard of the Assemblies of God.

title: Concilio Tridentino, Sessio XIII, De Eucharistia
titleEn: Council of Trent, Session 13, Decree on the Eucharist
source: In sanctissimo Eucharistiae sacramento est vere, realiter et substantialiter corpus et sanguis, una cum anima et divinitate Domini nostri Iesu Christi, atque adeo totus Christus. Sub panis specie tota substantia panis in corpus Christi transubstantiatur.
sourceEn: In the most holy sacrament of the Eucharist there is truly, really, and substantially contained the body and blood, together with the soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ, and consequently the whole Christ. Under the species of bread the whole substance of bread is transubstantiated into the body of Christ.
excerpt: 지극히 거룩한 성체 성사 안에는 참되고 실체적으로 주 예수 그리스도의 몸과 피가 영혼과 신성과 함께 포함되어 있으며, 따라서 그리스도 전체가 있습니다. 빵의 표상 아래 빵의 전체 실체가 그리스도의 몸으로 변환됩니다.
excerptEn: In the most holy sacrament of the Eucharist there is truly, really, and substantially contained the body and blood, together with the soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ, and consequently the whole Christ.
provenance: Catholic Church, Council of Trent, Session 13, Decree on the Most Holy Eucharist (1551). Authoritative conciliar definition of the doctrine of transubstantiation.

title: Православный Катехизис Филарета, О Евхаристии
titleEn: Catechism of Philaret (Drozdov), On the Eucharist
source: В Евхаристии мы принимаем истинное Тело и истинную Кровь Христовы, под видом хлеба и вина. Сие есть пресуществление, которое совершается призыванием Святаго Духа.
sourceEn: In the Eucharist we receive the true Body and true Blood of Christ, under the form of bread and wine. This is transubstantiation, which is accomplished by the invocation of the Holy Spirit.
excerpt: 성찬에서 우리는 빵과 포도주의 형상 아래 그리스도의 참 몸과 참 피를 받습니다. 이것은 성령의 청하심으로 이루어지는 변환입니다.
excerptEn: In the Eucharist we receive the true Body and true Blood of Christ, under the form of bread and wine. This is transubstantiation, which is accomplished by the invocation of the Holy Spirit.
provenance: Philaret (Drozdov), Metropolitan of Moscow, Orthodox Catechism, On the Eucharist (1830). Eastern Orthodox catechetical standard.

title: Confessio Fidei Westmonasteriensis, Caput XXIX
titleEn: Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 29
source: The Lord's Supper is a sacrament of the New Testament, wherein, by giving and receiving bread and wine according to the appointment of Jesus Christ, His death is showed forth; and the worthy receivers are, not after a corporal and carnal manner, but by faith, made partakers of His body and blood.
sourceEn: The Lord's Supper is a sacrament of the New Testament, wherein, by giving and receiving bread and wine according to the appointment of Jesus Christ, His death is showed forth; and the worthy receivers are, not after a corporal and carnal manner, but by faith, made partakers of His body and blood.
excerpt: 주의 만찬은 새 언약의 성례입니다. 예수 그리스도의 지정에 따라 빵과 포도주를 주고받음으로써 그분의 죽음이 나타나며, 합당한 받는 자들은 육체적 방식이 아니라 믿음으로 그분의 몸과 피에 참여하는 자가 됩니다.
excerptEn: The Lord's Supper is a sacrament of the New Testament, wherein, by giving and receiving bread and wine, His death is showed forth; and worthy receivers are, by faith, made partakers of His body and blood.
provenance: Westminster Assembly, Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 29: Of the Lord's Supper (1646). Reformed confessional standard.

title: Confessio Augustana, Articulus X
titleEn: Augsburg Confession, Article X
source: De coena Domini docent quod corpus et sanguis Christi vere adsint et distribuantur vescentibus in coena Domini, et improbant secus docentes.
sourceEn: Of the Supper of the Lord they teach that the true body and blood of Christ are truly present under the form of bread and wine in the Lord's Supper, and are there distributed and received; and they reject those who teach otherwise.
excerpt: 주의 만찬에 대해 그들은 그리스도의 참 몸과 참 피가 주의 만찬에서 빵과 포도주의 형상 아래 참으로 임하며, 거기서 분배되고 받는다고 가르칩니다.
excerptEn: Of the Supper of the Lord they teach that the true body and blood of Christ are truly present under the form of bread and wine in the Lord's Supper, and are there distributed and received.
provenance: Philip Melanchthon, Augsburg Confession, Article X: Of the Supper of the Lord (1530). Primary Lutheran confessional document.

title: The Baptist Faith and Message, Article VII
titleEn: Baptist Faith and Message 2000, Article VII: The Lord's Supper
source: The Lord's Supper is a symbolic act of obedience whereby members of the church, through partaking of the bread and the fruit of the vine, memorialize the death of the Redeemer and anticipate His second coming.
sourceEn: The Lord's Supper is a symbolic act of obedience whereby members of the church, through partaking of the bread and the fruit of the vine, memorialize the death of the Redeemer and anticipate His second coming.
excerpt: 주의 만찬은 교회 구성원들이 빵과 포도즙을 나눔으로써 구속주의 죽음을 기념하고 그분의 재림을 기대하는 상징적 순종의 행위입니다. 이것은 그리스도의 죽으심을 기념하고 그분의 재림을 기대하는 순종의 상징적 행위입니다.
excerptEn: The Lord's Supper is a symbolic act of obedience whereby members of the church, through partaking of the bread and the fruit of the vine, memorialize the death of the Redeemer and anticipate His second coming.
provenance: Southern Baptist Convention, The Baptist Faith and Message, Article VII (2000). Official statement emphasizing memorial and symbolic nature of the Lord's Supper.

title: Articles of Religion, Article XVIII
titleEn: Articles of Religion (Methodist), Article XVIII: Of the Lord's Supper
source: The Supper of the Lord is not only a sign of the love that Christians ought to have among themselves one to another, but rather is a sacrament of our redemption by Christ's death: insomuch that to such as rightly, worthily, and with faith receive the same, the bread which we break is a partaking of the body of Christ.
sourceEn: The Supper of the Lord is not only a sign of the love that Christians ought to have among themselves one to another, but rather is a sacrament of our redemption by Christ's death: insomuch that to such as rightly, worthily, and with faith receive the same, the bread which we break is a partaking of the body of Christ.
excerpt: 주의 만찬은 그리스도인들이 서로 가져야 할 사랑의 표징일 뿐만 아니라, 그리스도의 죽으심으로 이루신 우리의 구속의 성사입니다. 올바르고 합당하며 믿음으로 받는 자들에게 우리가 떼는 빵은 그리스도의 몸에 참여하는 것입니다.
excerptEn: The Supper of the Lord is not only a sign of the love that Christians ought to have among themselves, but rather is a sacrament of our redemption by Christ's death.
provenance: John Wesley, Articles of Religion (Methodist), Article XVIII: Of the Lord's Supper (1784). Adapted from the Anglican Thirty-Nine Articles for Methodism.

title: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 6
titleEn: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 6: The Lord's Supper
source: The Lord's Supper, consisting of the elements of bread and the fruit of the vine, is the symbol expressing our sharing the divine nature of our Lord Jesus Christ; a memorial of His suffering and death; and a prophecy of His second coming.
sourceEn: The Lord's Supper, consisting of the elements of bread and the fruit of the vine, is the symbol expressing our sharing the divine nature of our Lord Jesus Christ; a memorial of His suffering and death; and a prophecy of His second coming.
excerpt: 주의 만찬은 빵과 포도즙의 요소로 구성되며, 주 예수 그리스도의 신성에 참여하는 것을 표현하는 상징이며, 그분의 고난과 죽으심의 기념이며, 그분의 재림의 예언입니다.
excerptEn: The Lord's Supper is the symbol expressing our sharing the divine nature of our Lord Jesus Christ; a memorial of His suffering and death; and a prophecy of His second coming.
provenance: General Council of the Assemblies of God, Statement of Faith, Article 6 (1916). Emphasizes memorial and symbolic understanding of the Lord's Supper.

title: Concilio Tridentino, Sessio VI, De Iustificatione
titleEn: Council of Trent, Session 6, Decree on Justification
source: Nemo potest esse certus de perseverantia sine speciali revelatione. Quamvis enim sperare liceat, tamen certitudo perseverantiae ex fide catholica non potest haberi.
sourceEn: No one can be certain of perseverance without special revelation. For although one may hope, the certainty of perseverance cannot be had from the Catholic faith.
excerpt: 특별한 계시 없이는 아무도 견인의 확신을 가질 수 없습니다. 소망할 수는 있지만, 견인의 확실성은 가톨릭 신앙에서 올 수 없습니다.
excerptEn: No one can be certain of perseverance without special revelation. For although one may hope, the certainty of perseverance cannot be had from the Catholic faith.
provenance: Catholic Church, Council of Trent, Session 6, Decree on Justification, Chapter 13 and Canon 16 (1547). Rejects the certainty of final perseverance without special revelation.

title: Катехизис Восточной Церкви, О Спасении
titleEn: Catechism of the Eastern Church, On Salvation
source: Спасение есть процесс обожения, который продолжается всю жизнь. Никто не может утверждать, что он уже спасён, ибо сие зависит от постоянного сотрудничества с благодатью Божией до конца.
sourceEn: Salvation is a lifelong process of theosis, of becoming partakers of the divine nature. No one can claim to be already saved, for this depends on continual cooperation with the grace of God to the end.
excerpt: 구원은 신화(참여), 즉 신성한 본성에 참여하게 되는 평생의 과정입니다. 아무도 이미 구원받았다고 주장할 수 없으며, 이는 끝까지 하나님의 은혜와의 지속적인 협력에 달려 있습니다.
excerptEn: Salvation is a lifelong process of theosis, of becoming partakers of the divine nature. No one can claim to be already saved, for this depends on continual cooperation with the grace of God to the end.
provenance: Eastern Orthodox theological tradition as expressed in catechetical teaching on theosis. Reflects the patristic understanding of salvation as an ongoing synergistic process.

title: Confessio Fidei Westmonasteriensis, Caput XVII
titleEn: Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 17
source: They whom God hath accepted in His Beloved, effectually called and sanctified by His Spirit, can neither totally nor finally fall away from the state of grace; but shall certainly persevere therein to the end, and be eternally saved.
sourceEn: They whom God hath accepted in His Beloved, effectually called and sanctified by His Spirit, can neither totally nor finally fall away from the state of grace; but shall certainly persevere therein to the end, and be eternally saved.
excerpt: 하나님이 그의 사랑하시는 자 안에서 받아들이시고, 그의 영으로 효력 있게 부르시고 거룩하게 하신 자들은 은혜의 상태에서 전적으로 또는 최종적으로 떨어질 수 없으며, 끝까지 확실히 그 안에서 견인하고 영원히 구원을 받을 것입니다.
excerptEn: They whom God hath accepted in His Beloved can neither totally nor finally fall away from the state of grace; but shall certainly persevere therein to the end, and be eternally saved.
provenance: Westminster Assembly, Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 17: Of the Perseverance of the Saints (1646). Classic Reformed statement of the perseverance of the elect.

title: Formula Concordiae, Articulus XI, De Aeterna Praedestinatione
titleEn: Formula of Concord, Article XI: Of God's Eternal Election
source: Eterna Dei electio est causa salutis nostrae, quam Deus ante mundi constitutionem in Christo constituit. Haec electio certa est et immutabilis, quia in Christo fundatur.
sourceEn: The eternal election of God is the cause of our salvation, which God established in Christ before the foundation of the world. This election is certain and unchangeable, because it is founded in Christ.
excerpt: 하나님의 영원한 선택은 우리의 구원의 원인으로, 하나님이 세상 창조 전에 그리스도 안에서 정하셨습니다. 이 선택은 그리스도 안에 기반을 두고 있으므로 확실하고 변하지 않습니다.
excerptEn: The eternal election of God is the cause of our salvation, which God established in Christ before the foundation of the world. This election is certain and unchangeable, because it is founded in Christ.
provenance: Formula of Concord, Article XI: Of God's Eternal Election (1577). Part of the Lutheran Book of Concord; affirms election grounded in Christ as the basis of salvation security.

title: The Baptist Faith and Message, Article V
titleEn: Baptist Faith and Message 2000, Article V: God's Purpose of Grace
source: All true believers endure to the end. Those whom God has accepted in Christ, and sanctified by His Spirit, will never fall away from the state of grace, but shall persevere to the end.
sourceEn: All true believers endure to the end. Those whom God has accepted in Christ, and sanctified by His Spirit, will never fall away from the state of grace, but shall persevere to the end.
excerpt: 모든 참된 신자는 끝까지 견딥니다. 그리스도 안에서 하나님이 받아들이시고 그의 영으로 거룩하게 하신 자들은 은혜의 상태에서 결코 떨어지지 않고 끝까지 견딜 것입니다.
excerptEn: All true believers endure to the end. Those whom God has accepted in Christ, and sanctified by His Spirit, will never fall away from the state of grace, but shall persevere to the end.
provenance: Southern Baptist Convention, The Baptist Faith and Message, Article V: God's Purpose of Grace (2000). Affirms eternal security of genuine believers.

title: Articles of Religion, Article XII
titleEn: Articles of Religion (Methodist), Article XII: Of Sin After Baptism
source: After we have received the Holy Ghost, we may depart from grace given and fall into sin, and by the grace of God we may arise again and amend our lives. And therefore they are to be condemned which say they can no more sin as long as they live here.
sourceEn: After we have received the Holy Ghost, we may depart from grace given and fall into sin, and by the grace of God we may arise again and amend our lives.
excerpt: 성령을 받은 후에도 우리는 주어진 은혜에서 떠나 죄에 빠질 수 있으며, 하나님의 은혜로 다시 일어나 삶을 개선할 수 있습니다. 따라서 살아 있는 동안 더 이상 죄를 짓지 않는다고 말하는 자들은 정죄받아야 합니다.
excerptEn: After we have received the Holy Ghost, we may depart from grace given and fall into sin, and by the grace of God we may arise again and amend our lives.
provenance: John Wesley, Articles of Religion (Methodist), Article XII: Of Sin After Baptism (1784). Expresses the Arminian-Wesleyan view that believers may fall from grace.

title: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 5
titleEn: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 5: The Fall of Man
source: We believe the only means of being cleansed from sin is through repentance and faith in the precious blood of Christ. Regeneration by the Holy Spirit is absolutely essential for personal salvation.
sourceEn: We believe the only means of being cleansed from sin is through repentance and faith in the precious blood of Christ. Regeneration by the Holy Spirit is absolutely essential for personal salvation.
excerpt: 우리는 죄에서 정결하게 되는 유일한 방법이 회개와 그리스도의 보배로운 피에 대한 믿음을 통해서라고 믿습니다. 성령에 의한 거듭남은 개인 구원에 절대적으로 필수적입니다.
excerptEn: The only means of being cleansed from sin is through repentance and faith in the precious blood of Christ. Regeneration by the Holy Spirit is absolutely essential for personal salvation.
provenance: General Council of the Assemblies of God, Statement of Faith, Article 5 (1916). Emphasizes the necessity of repentance and faith for salvation, with ongoing sanctification.

title: Catechismus Catholicae Ecclesiae, §600
titleEn: Catechism of the Catholic Church, §600
source: Deus neminem praedestinat ad infernum. Causa damnationis est voluntas hominis a Deo aversa, non praedestinatio divina. Deus vult omnes homines salvos fieri.
sourceEn: God predestines no one to go to hell. The cause of damnation is the human will turned away from God, not divine predestination. God wills all people to be saved.
excerpt: 하나님은 아무도 지옥에 가도록 예정하지 않습니다. 멸망의 원인은 신적 예정이 아니라 하나님에게서 돌아선 인간의 의지입니다. 하나님은 모든 사람이 구원받기를 원하십니다.
excerptEn: God predestines no one to go to hell. The cause of damnation is the human will turned away from God, not divine predestination. God wills all people to be saved.
provenance: Catechism of the Catholic Church, §598-600 (1992). Authoritative catechetical teaching on predestination and the universal salvific will of God.

title: Μητροπολίτης Ναυπάκτου, Περί Προορισμού
titleEn: Metropolitan of Nafpaktos, On Predestination
source: Божие предопределение не абсолютно, а условно. Бог предвидит свободное произволение каждого человека и в соответствии с этим определяет участь каждого. Человек есть субъект своей судьбы.
sourceEn: Predestination is not absolute but conditional. God foresees the free will of each person and accordingly determines each one's destiny. Man is the subject of his own fate.
excerpt: 예정은 절대적이 아니라 조건적입니다. 하나님은 각 사람의 자유 의지를 미리 아시고 그에 따라 각 사람의 운명을 결정하십니다. 사람은 자신의 운명의 주체입니다.
excerptEn: Predestination is not absolute but conditional. God foresees the free will of each person and accordingly determines each one's destiny. Man is the subject of his own fate.
provenance: Metropolitan Hierotheos (Vlachos) of Nafpaktos, Orthodox patristic teaching on predestination. Reflects the Eastern Orthodox emphasis on synergy between divine foreknowledge and human free will.

title: Canones Synodi Dordrechtanae, Caput Primum
titleEn: Canons of Dort, First Head of Doctrine
source: God has chosen certain persons before the foundation of the world out of the common mass of sinners, and has decreed to give them to Christ to be saved by Him. This election is not founded upon foreseen faith or works, but solely upon the good pleasure of God.
sourceEn: God has chosen certain persons before the foundation of the world out of the common mass of sinners, and has decreed to give them to Christ to be saved by Him. This election is not founded upon foreseen faith or works, but solely upon the good pleasure of God.
excerpt: 하나님은 세상 기초 전에 죄인들의 공통된 무리 중에서 특정인들을 택하시고, 그리스도께 주어 구원받도록 정하셨습니다. 이 선택은 미리 본 믿음이나 행위에 기초하지 않고 오직 하나님의 선한 기쁘심에만 근거합니다.
excerptEn: God has chosen certain persons before the foundation of the world out of the common mass of sinners. This election is not founded upon foreseen faith or works, but solely upon the good pleasure of God.
provenance: Synod of Dort, Canons of Dort, First Head of Doctrine: Divine Election and Reprobation (1619). Reformed conciliar response to the Arminian Remonstrants.

title: Formula Concordiae, Articulus XI
titleEn: Formula of Concord, Article XI: Of God's Eternal Election
source: God's eternal election, however, not only foresees and foreknows the salvation of the elect, but by God's gracious will and pleasure in Christ Jesus is also a cause which creates, effects, helps, and furthers our salvation and whatever belongs to it.
sourceEn: God's eternal election not only foresees and foreknows the salvation of the elect, but by God's gracious will and pleasure in Christ Jesus is also a cause which creates, effects, helps, and furthers our salvation.
excerpt: 하나님의 영원한 선택은 택하신 자들의 구원을 미리 보고 아실 뿐만 아니라, 그리스도 예수 안에서 하나님의 은혜로운 뜻과 기쁨으로 우리의 구원을 창조하고, 효과를 내고, 돕고, 촉진하는 원인이기도 합니다.
excerptEn: God's eternal election not only foresees and foreknows the salvation of the elect, but is also a cause which creates, effects, helps, and furthers our salvation.
provenance: Formula of Concord, Article XI: Of God's Eternal Election (1577). Lutheran confessional teaching distinguishing Lutheran predestination from Calvinist predestination.

title: The Baptist Faith and Message, Article V
titleEn: Baptist Faith and Message 2000, Article V: God's Purpose of Grace
source: Election is the gracious purpose of God, according to which He regenerates, justifies, sanctifies, and glorifies sinners. It is consistent with the free agency of man, and comprehends all the means in connection with the end.
sourceEn: Election is the gracious purpose of God, according to which He regenerates, justifies, sanctifies, and glorifies sinners. It is consistent with the free agency of man, and comprehends all the means in connection with the end.
excerpt: 선택은 하나님이 죄인들을 거듭나게 하시고, 의롭게 하시고, 거룩하게 하시고, 영화롭게 하시는 은혜로운 목적입니다. 이것은 인간의 자유 의지와 일치하며, 목적과 관련된 모든 수단을 포함합니다.
excerptEn: Election is the gracious purpose of God, according to which He regenerates, justifies, sanctifies, and glorifies sinners. It is consistent with the free agency of man.
provenance: Southern Baptist Convention, The Baptist Faith and Message, Article V: God's Purpose of Grace (2000). Balances divine election with human responsibility.

title: Articles of Religion, Articles on Predestination
titleEn: Articles of Religion (Methodist), On Predestination and Election
source: The condition of man after the fall of Adam is such that he cannot turn and prepare himself by his own natural strength and good works to faith and calling upon God. Wherefore we have no power to do good works without the grace of God preventing us.
sourceEn: The condition of man after the fall of Adam is such that he cannot turn and prepare himself by his own natural strength. Wherefore we have no power without the grace of God preventing us, but God's grace is resistible.
excerpt: 아담의 타락 이후 인간의 상태는 자신의 자연적 힘과 선한 행위로 믿음과 하나님 부르심을 향해 돌이키고 준비할 수 없는 것입니다. 하나님의 은혜 없이는 선을 행할 능력이 없지만, 하나님의 은혜는 저항받을 수 있습니다.
excerptEn: The condition of man after the fall of Adam is such that he cannot turn and prepare himself by his own natural strength. God's grace is resistible, and election is conditional upon foreseen faith.
provenance: John Wesley, Articles of Religion (Methodist), adapted from the Anglican Articles. Expresses the Arminian-Wesleyan understanding of conditional election and resistible grace.

title: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 4
titleEn: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 4: The Fall of Man
source: We believe in divine election according to the foreknowledge of God. Election is based upon God's foreknowledge of who would believe, not upon His sovereign choice apart from human response.
sourceEn: We believe in divine election according to the foreknowledge of God. Election is based upon God's foreknowledge of who would believe, not upon His sovereign choice apart from human response.
excerpt: 우리는 하나님의 예지에 따른 신적 선택을 믿습니다. 선택은 인간의 반응과 별개의 주권적 선택에 근거하지 않고, 누가 믿을 것인지에 대한 하나님의 예지에 근거합니다.
excerptEn: We believe in divine election according to the foreknowledge of God. Election is based upon God's foreknowledge of who would believe, not upon His sovereign choice apart from human response.
provenance: General Council of the Assemblies of God, Statement of Faith (1916). Affirms foreknowledge-based election, consistent with Arminian-Wesleyan theology common in Pentecostal traditions.

title: Catechismus Catholicae Ecclesiae, §2003
titleEn: Catechism of the Catholic Church, §2003
source: Gratia est gratuitum et indebitum auxilium quod Deus nobis ad salutem praebet. Sanctificantis gratiae dispositiones sunt dona Spiritus Sancti, quae fideles ad opera sancta movent.
sourceEn: Grace is the free and undeserved help that God gives us to respond to his call to become children of God. The special graces of the Holy Spirit's gifts dispose Christians to follow the promptings of the Holy Spirit.
excerpt: 은혜는 하나님이 우리에게 주시는 무료이며 받을 자격 없는 도움입니다. 성령의 은사는 그리스도인들이 성령의 인도를 따르도록 준비시키는 성화의 은혜입니다.
excerptEn: Grace is the free and undeserved help that God gives us to respond to his call. The special graces of the Holy Spirit's gifts dispose Christians to follow the promptings of the Holy Spirit.
provenance: Catechism of the Catholic Church, §1996-2005 (1992). Teaches on grace, the gifts of the Holy Spirit, and their role in the Christian life.

title: Православный Катехизис, О Дарах Святаго Духа
titleEn: Catechism of the Orthodox Church, On the Gifts of the Holy Spirit
source: Дары Святаго Духа даются для созидания Церкви и для освящения верных. Каждый верный получает дарования по мере своей веры и для общей пользы тела Христова.
sourceEn: The gifts of the Holy Spirit are given for the building up of the Church and for the sanctification of the faithful. Each believer receives gifts according to the measure of faith and for the common good of the body of Christ.
excerpt: 성령의 은사는 교회를 세우고 신자들을 거룩하게 하기 위해 주어집니다. 각 신자는 믿음의 분량에 따라 그리스도의 몸의 공동 유익을 위해 은사를 받습니다.
excerptEn: The gifts of the Holy Spirit are given for the building up of the Church and for the sanctification of the faithful. Each believer receives gifts according to the measure of faith and for the common good.
provenance: Eastern Orthodox catechetical tradition, as expressed in the Catechism of Philaret and patristic teaching. Emphasizes gifts for communal edification within the body of Christ.

title: Confessio Fidei Westmonasteriensis, Caput I
titleEn: Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 1
source: The gifts of the Spirit are given for the edification of the Church and the advancement of the gospel. The Holy Spirit speaking in the Scriptures is the supreme judge by which all controversies of religion are to be determined.
sourceEn: The gifts of the Spirit are given for the edification of the Church and the advancement of the gospel. The Holy Spirit speaking in the Scriptures is the supreme judge by which all controversies of religion are to be determined.
excerpt: 성령의 은사는 교회의 덕을 세우고 복음의 진전을 위해 주어집니다. 성경에서 말씀하시는 성령은 모든 종교적 논쟁이 결정되어야 할 최고의 심판자입니다.
excerptEn: The gifts of the Spirit are given for the edification of the Church and the advancement of the gospel. The Holy Spirit speaking in the Scriptures is the supreme judge.
provenance: Westminster Assembly, Westminster Confession of Faith, Chapter 1: Of the Holy Scripture (1646). Reformed emphasis on the sufficiency of Scripture for the governance of spiritual gifts.

title: Confessio Augustana, Articulus V
titleEn: Augsburg Confession, Article V
source: Ut hanc fidem consequamur, ministerium docendi et administrandi sacramenta institutum est. Nam per verbum et sacramenta, tamquam per instrumenta, donatur Spiritus Sanctus, qui fidem efficit.
sourceEn: To obtain such faith God instituted the office of the ministry, that is, provided the Gospel and the sacraments. Through these, as through means, He gives the Holy Spirit, who works faith when and where He pleases.
excerpt: 이런 믿음을 얻기 위해 하나님은 성직을 제정하셨습니다. 즉 복음과 성례전을 통해 성령을 주시며, 성령은 원하실 때 어디서든 믿음을 일으키십니다.
excerptEn: To obtain such faith God instituted the office of the ministry. Through Word and Sacraments the Holy Spirit is given, who works faith when and where He pleases.
provenance: Philip Melanchthon, Augsburg Confession, Article V: Of the Ministry (1530). Lutheran confessional teaching on the means of grace through which the Holy Spirit distributes gifts.

title: The Baptist Faith and Message, Article I
titleEn: Baptist Faith and Message 2000, Article I: The Scriptures
source: The Holy Spirit gives gifts for service. All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for rebuking, for correcting, for training in righteousness, so that the person of God may be complete, equipped for every good work.
sourceEn: The Holy Spirit gives gifts for service. All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for rebuking, for correcting, for training in righteousness, so that the person of God may be complete, equipped for every good work.
excerpt: 성령은 봉사를 위해 은사를 주십니다. 모든 성경은 하나님의 영감으로 된 것으로 교훈과 책망과 바르게 함과 의로 훈련하기에 유익하여, 하나님의 사람이 온전하여 모든 선한 일을 행할 능력을 갖추게 합니다.
excerptEn: The Holy Spirit gives gifts for service. All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for rebuking, for correcting, for training in righteousness.
provenance: Southern Baptist Convention, The Baptist Faith and Message, Article I: The Scriptures (2000). Emphasizes Spirit-given gifts for service grounded in Scripture.

title: Articles of Religion, On the Holy Spirit
titleEn: Articles of Religion (Methodist), On the Gifts of the Spirit
source: The gifts of the Spirit are for today and are given to every believer for the common good of the Church. The Holy Spirit distributes gifts severally as He wills, empowering believers for service and witness.
sourceEn: The gifts of the Spirit are for today and are given to every believer for the common good of the Church. The Holy Spirit distributes gifts severally as He wills, empowering believers for service and witness.
excerpt: 성령의 은사는 오늘날에도 있으며 교회의 공동 유익을 위해 모든 신자에게 주어집니다. 성령은 원하시는 대로 은사를 나누어 주시며 신자들에게 봉사와 증거의 능력을 주십니다.
excerptEn: The gifts of the Spirit are for today and are given to every believer for the common good of the Church. The Holy Spirit distributes gifts severally as He wills.
provenance: John Wesley and the Methodist tradition, Articles of Religion. Affirms the continuation of spiritual gifts, reflecting Wesley's emphasis on experiential faith and the work of the Holy Spirit.

title: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Article 7 and 8
titleEn: Assemblies of God Statement of Faith, Articles 7-8: The Baptism in the Holy Spirit and Evidence of Speaking in Tongues
source: We believe in the present-day operation of the gifts of the Spirit, including speaking in tongues, prophecy, healing, and other supernatural manifestations. The baptism of believers in the Holy Spirit is witnessed by the initial physical sign of speaking with other tongues.
sourceEn: We believe in the present-day operation of the gifts of the Spirit, including speaking in tongues, prophecy, healing, and other supernatural manifestations. The baptism of believers in the Holy Spirit is witnessed by the initial physical sign of speaking with other tongues.
excerpt: 우리는 방언, 예언, 치유 및 기타 초자연적 나타남을 포함한 성령 은사의 현재 운영을 믿습니다. 성령에 의한 신자들의 세례는 다른 방언으로 말하는 초기 물리적 표적으로 입증됩니다.
excerptEn: We believe in the present-day operation of the gifts of the Spirit, including speaking in tongues, prophecy, healing, and other supernatural manifestations.
provenance: General Council of the Assemblies of God, Statement of Faith, Articles 7-8 (1916). Central Pentecostal distinctive affirming the continuation and operation of all spiritual gifts, with speaking in tongues as the initial evidence of Spirit baptism.
